core_content.markdown:
(2nd leg: February 5. St James' Park)
won,Arsenal: Alexander Isak, Newcastle's supreme centre forward who is emerging as one of the deadliest goalscorers in Europe. c AndAnthonyGordon-the multi-faceted England winger whose reputation as one of this countrys most impressive attacking talents is burgeoning. over the past 12 months. Both struck ruthlessly here to plunge a dagger into the Gunners'hopes of winning the Carabao Cup for the first time in 32vears. being Arsenal—willbelieve they can pulloffthe turnaround at St James'Parkin the secondleg next month. But on the evidence of this showing, they haven't got a cat in hell's chance.Notifthey etIsakrunriotlikehe didhere. That's not to say this was a one-man show.Farfromit. This was a Newcastle away performance oursting with ability, discipline,work ethic and pride.Isak's showing was merely the cing on the cake.Arsenal willknow they will need to find an xtra gear—maybe two—in the secondleg.This was not a display in keeping with the eputation Mikel Arteta has built here. nead north with hope in their hearts.This vas a bruising night for them,but not fatal;hey will cling on to that optimism. s various guises over the years.People even ay playing at Wembley doesn't mean as nuch as it once did.Try telling thatto the 2 nlayers who started the game here. mirates crowd who were gripped and yped from the very start. # Howe puts one foot i1 final as Arteta hits a black-and-white wall imber,William Saliba,Lewis Iall andGabrielMartinelli,who ere all agonisedafter missing rst-half opportunities to open he scoring. id open the scoring. Winning rophies matters and playing embley is as magical as it ver was. as rip-roaring. Declan Rice's nswinging corners were causing Tewcastleallmanneroftrouble ndTimber should really have ispatched a header in the13th ninute from his team-mate's hinned set-piece.o For the opening 20 minutes,was all hands to pump for the eordies as Arsenaltried to seize ae initiative.c But gradually Newcastle grew.s did the decibel level from ne away end after Halls effort as blocked following brilliant Arsenal's early flow had been tifled.Joelinton's clearremitof ticking like glue to Arsenal aptain Martin Odegaardwas ntegral to Newcastle finding air feet ene he post with just Martin Dubravka to beat following Leandro Trossard's sumptuous touch in the 29th minute,perhaps the opening 45 minutes would have gone in Arsenal's favour.It didn't.though -thanks to Isak. Newcastle's opener was route-one football at its most effective.Dubravka's drilled punt forward was flickedonby Sven Botman, Jacob Murphy bundled it on and the ball was hammered home byIsak. is no secret. If the Gunners can make the finances work there is every chance Isak will be wheeling away in celebration at the Emirates more frequently next season.But that's for then.The present is far more pressing. thwarted towards the end of the first half as Arsenal strived to pulllevel. half-time became abundantly clear during the opening the stages of the second period. The pace was slower, far more controlledby the Gunners.Like chalk and cheese in comparison to the whirlwind first half. It made sense for Arsenalto take the sting out of the game.Going toe-to-toe with Newcastle did not reap the rewards they had hoped for in the first half. second-half approach.Again Isak took centre stage when somehow,with three Arsenal players around him,he forced David Raya into a save to leave Gordon the simple c Newcastle were euphoric.It's a long journey down to Londo from up there but the return tri will not seem guite so arduous. deal with Isak.Saliba and Gabrie are a formidablepairing but the striker was making them look ordinary here. opportunity just before the hour to half the deficit.
